rationale.fidelity: Most mappings are correct, but the final business problem tells the planner to choose an uncertainty budget even though the supplied decision variables contain only supplier order quantities. That materially adds a decision without declaring an extension.
rationale.managerial_framing: The response otherwise gives a strong protection-versus-cost trade-off, mechanism, and bounded guarantee, but the added budget decision changes the managerial problem.
rationale.scholarly_positioning: It makes no unsupported novelty claim and appropriately limits itself to model-grounded framing.
rationale.evidence_discipline: Calibration is identified as an assumption requiring evidence and out-of-set guarantees are rejected; the undeclared decision extension remains the material defect.
rationale.prose_clarity: The structure is thorough, but recurring mojibake and the internally inconsistent treatment of the uncertainty budget reduce clarity.
rationale.overall: Raw quality is strong, but adding the uncertainty budget as a controlled choice triggers the noncompensatory undeclared-model-change failure, so the score is capped below passing.
